mnemonics: SCOOP Rules: Sequentially Cover One Outstanding Pattern. A greedy algorithm that scoops up the best rule for the current data, removes the covered examples, and repeats.
keyPoints: A greedy, iterative algorithm that learns one rule at a time.
After a rule is learned, the examples it covers (correctly classifies) are removed from the training set.
The process repeats on the remaining, 'uncovered' examples until a stopping criterion is met.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the algorithm is sensitive to the order of examples/classes, which can lead to suboptimal rule sets.
Confusing it with a 'separate-and-conquer' strategy instead of the more general 'divide-and-conquer' used in decision trees.
